heading: Regulation of futures trading and foreign transactions

children.text: such transaction is conducted on or subject to the rules of a board of trade which has been designated or registered by the Commission as a contract market or derivatives transaction execution facility for such commodity;
such contract is executed or consummated by or through a contract market; and

such contract is evidenced by a record in writing which shows the date, the parties to such contract and their addresses, the property covered and its price, and the terms of delivery: , That each contract market or derivatives transaction execution facility member shall keep such record for a period of three years from the date thereof, or for a longer period if the Commission shall so direct, which record shall at all times be open to the inspection of any representative of the Commission or the Department of Justice.Provided

children.heading: Regulation of foreign transactions by United States persons
children.label: b Regulation of foreign transactions by United States persons
i
children.text: whether any such foreign board of trade is subject to comparable, comprehensive supervision and regulation by the appropriate governmental authorities in the foreign board of trade’s home country; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any previous commission findings that the foreign board of trade is subject to comparable comprehensive supervision and regulation by the appropriate government authorities in the foreign board of trade’s home country.1

children.label: i
children.text: the foreign board of trade makes public daily trading information regarding the agreement, contract, or transaction that is comparable to the daily trading information published by the registered entity for the 1 or more contracts against which the agreement, contract, or transaction traded on the foreign board of trade settles; and

children.label: I
children.text: adopts position limits (including related hedge exemption provisions) for the agreement, contract, or transaction that are comparable to the position limits (including related hedge exemption provisions) adopted by the registered entity for the 1 or more contracts against which the agreement, contract, or transaction traded on the foreign board of trade settles;

children.label: II
children.text: has the authority to require or direct market participants to limit, reduce, or liquidate any position the foreign board of trade (or the foreign futures authority that oversees the foreign board of trade) determines to be necessary to prevent or reduce the threat of price manipulation, excessive speculation as described in , price distortion, or disruption of delivery or the cash settlement process;section 6a of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: the information that the foreign board of trade will make publicly available;

children.label: bb
children.text: the position limits that the foreign board of trade or foreign futures authority will adopt and enforce;

children.label: cc
children.text: the position reductions required to prevent manipulation, excessive speculation as described in , price distortion, or disruption of delivery or the cash settlement process; andsection 6a of this title

children.label: dd
children.text: any other area of interest expressed by the Commission to the foreign board of trade or foreign futures authority;

children.label: IV
children.text: provides information to the Commission regarding large trader positions in the agreement, contract, or transaction that is comparable to the large trader position information collected by the Commission for the 1 or more contracts against which the agreement, contract, or transaction traded on the foreign board of trade settles; and

children.label: V
children.text: provides the Commission such information as is necessary to publish reports on aggregate trader positions for the agreement, contract, or transaction traded on the foreign board of trade that are comparable to such reports on aggregate trader positions for the 1 or more contracts against which the agreement, contract, or transaction traded on the foreign board of trade settles.

children.heading: Existing foreign boards of trade
children.label: C Existing foreign boards of trade
children.text: Subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall not be effective with respect to any foreign board of trade to which, prior to , the Commission granted direct access permission until the date that is 180 days after .July 21, 2010July 21, 2010

children.heading: Persons located in the United States
children.label: 2 Persons located in the United States

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission may adopt rules and regulations proscribing fraud and requiring minimum financial standards, the disclosure of risk, the filing of reports, the keeping of books and records, the safeguarding of customers’ funds, and registration with the Commission by any person located in the United States, its territories or possessions, who engages in the offer or sale of any contract of sale of a commodity for future delivery that is made or to be made on or subject to the rules of a board of trade, exchange, or market located outside the United States, its territories or possessions.

children.heading: Different requirements
children.label: B Different requirements
children.text: Rules and regulations described in subparagraph (A) may impose different requirements for such persons depending upon the particular foreign board of trade, exchange, or market involved.

children.label: i
children.text: requires Commission approval of any contract, rule, regulation, or action of any foreign board of trade, exchange, or market, or clearinghouse for such board of trade, exchange, or market; or

children.label: ii
children.text: governs in any way any rule or contract term or action of any foreign board of trade, exchange, or market, or clearinghouse for such board of trade, exchange, or market.

children.label: I
children.text: paragraphs (2), (3), (4), (5), and (7), paragraph (18)(A)(vii)(III), paragraphs (23), (24), (31), (32), (38), (39), (41), (42), (46), (47), (48), and (49) of , and sections 2(a)(13), 2(c)(1)(D), 6a(a), 6a(b), 6d(c), 6d(d), 6r, 6s, 7a–1(a), 7a–1(b), 7(d), 7(g), 7(h), 7a–1(c), 7a–1(i), 12(e), and 24a of this title; andsection 1a of this title34So in original. does not contain subsecs. (g) and (h).3Section 7 of this titleSee References in Text note below.4

children.label: II
children.text: section 206(e) of the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (; note); and5Public Law 106–10215 U.S.C. 78cSo in original. Section 206 of the Act does not contain a subsec. (e).5

children.label: ii
children.text: in sections 721(c) and 742 of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Commission and the Securities and Exchange Commission may by rule, regulation, or order jointly exclude any agreement, contract, or transaction from ) if the Commissions determine that the exemption would be consistent with the public interest.section 2(a)(1)(D) of this title6So in original. The closing parenthesis probably should not appear.6

children.label: A
children.text: the requirement should not be applied to the agreement, contract, or transaction for which the exemption is sought and that the exemption would be consistent with the public interest and the purposes of this chapter; and

children.label: i
children.text: will be entered into solely between appropriate persons; and

children.label: ii
children.text: will not have a material adverse effect on the ability of the Commission or any contract market or derivatives transaction execution facility to discharge its regulatory or self-regulatory duties under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: A bank or trust company (acting in an individual or fiduciary capacity).

children.label: B
children.text: A savings association.

children.label: C
children.text: An insurance company.

children.label: D
children.text: An investment company subject to regulation under the Investment Company Act of 1940 ( et seq.).15 U.S.C. 80a–1

children.label: E
children.text: A commodity pool formed or operated by a person subject to regulation under this chapter.

children.label: F
children.text: A corporation, partnership, proprietorship, organization, trust, or other business entity with a net worth exceeding $1,000,000 or total assets exceeding $5,000,000, or the obligations of which under the agreement, contract or transaction are guaranteed or otherwise supported by a letter of credit or keepwell, support, or other agreement by any such entity or by an entity referred to in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), (H), (I), or (K) of this paragraph.

children.label: G
children.text: An employee benefit plan with assets exceeding $1,000,000, or whose investment decisions are made by a bank, trust company, insurance company, investment adviser registered under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 [ et seq.], or a commodity trading advisor subject to regulation under this chapter.15 U.S.C. 80b–1

children.label: H
children.text: Any governmental entity (including the United States, any state, or any foreign government) or political subdivision thereof, or any multinational or supranational entity or any instrumentality, agency, or department of any of the foregoing.7So in original. Probably should be capitalized.7

children.label: I
children.text: A broker-dealer subject to regulation under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( et seq.) acting on its own behalf or on behalf of another appropriate person.15 U.S.C. 78a

children.label: J
children.text: A futures commission merchant, floor broker, or floor trader subject to regulation under this chapter acting on its own behalf or on behalf of another appropriate person.

children.label: K
children.text: Such other persons that the Commission determines to be appropriate in light of their financial or other qualifications, or the applicability of appropriate regulatory protections.

children.label: A
children.text: the information sought to be restricted constitutes a trade secret; or

children.label: B
children.text: public disclosure of the information would result in material competitive harm to the applicant.

children.label: A
children.text: promptly following , or upon application by any person, exercise the exemptive authority granted under paragraph (1) with respect to classes of hybrid instruments that are predominantly securities or depository instruments, to the extent that such instruments may be regarded as subject to the provisions of this chapter; orOctober 28, 1992

children.label: B
children.text: promptly following , or upon application by any person, exercise the exemptive authority granted under paragraph (1) effective as of , with respect to classes of swap agreements (as defined in ) that are not part of a fungible class of agreements that are standardized as to their material economic terms, to the extent that such agreements may be regarded as subject to the provisions of this chapter.October 28, 1992October 23, 1974section 101 of title 11

children.label: A
children.text: pursuant to a tariff or rate schedule approved or permitted to take effect by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission;

children.label: B
children.text: pursuant to a tariff or rate schedule establishing rates or charges for, or protocols governing, the sale of electric energy approved or permitted to take effect by the regulatory authority of the State or municipality having jurisdiction to regulate rates and charges for the sale of electric energy within the State or municipality; or

children.label: C
children.text: between entities described in .section 824(f) of title 16

children.heading: Effect of exemption on investigative authority of Commission
children.label: d Effect of exemption on investigative authority of Commission
children.text: The granting of an exemption under this section shall not affect the authority of the Commission under any other provision of this chapter to conduct investigations in order to determine compliance with the requirements or conditions of such exemption or to take enforcement action for any violation of any provision of this chapter or any rule, regulation or order thereunder caused by the failure to comply with or satisfy such conditions or requirements.

children.heading: Liability of registered persons trading on a foreign board of trade
children.label: e Liability of registered persons trading on a foreign board of trade
i
children.text: legally organized under the laws of a foreign country;

children.label: ii
children.text: authorized to act as a board of trade by a foreign futures authority; and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to regulation by the foreign futures authority; and

children.label: B
children.text: has not been determined by the Commission to be operating in violation of subsection (a).

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as implying or creating any presumption that a board of trade, exchange, or market is located outside the United States, or its territories or possessions, for purposes of subsection (a).
